steps.action: Yarkhushta belongs to a wider category of Armeni with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Yarkhushta belongs to a wider category of Armeni with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: The dance is performed by men, who face each oth
steps.text: The dance is performed by men, who face each other in pairs.

steps.action: The key element of the dance is a forward moveme
steps.text: The key element of the dance is a forward movement where participants rapidly approach one another and vigorously clap onto the palms of hands of dancers in the opposite row.

steps.action: This factor amplifies the effect of adrenaline/e
steps.text: This factor amplifies the effect of adrenaline/epinephrine rush that the dancing of yarkhushta usually produces.

steps.action: The dance was popularized in the late 1930s by S
steps.text: The dance was popularized in the late 1930s by Srbuhi Lisitsian who taught at the Yerevan Dance College.

steps.action: In 1957, the dance underwent further choreograph
steps.text: In 1957, the dance underwent further choreographic refinement by folk culture enthusiast Vahram Aristakesian and was performed by a folk dance troupe from the village of Ashnak.

steps.action: The dance was revived in the 1980s by the folk g
steps.text: The dance was revived in the 1980s by the folk group Maratuk and, later, by the folk ensemble Karin.

steps.action: There are attempts to introduce yarkhushta into
steps.text: There are attempts to introduce yarkhushta into curriculum of dances and songs of the Armenian Army.

steps.action: Among them is the poem "Dance of Sassoun" («Սասո
steps.text: Among them is the poem "Dance of Sassoun" («Սասունցիների պարը») by Gevorg Emin published in 1975.

steps.action: There are several poems and samples of visual ar
steps.text: There are several poems and samples of visual art that touch on the theme of yarkhushta.

steps.action: Yarkhushta also features prominently in the film
steps.text: Yarkhushta also features prominently in the films Men («Տղամարդիկ», 1972) and Yarkhushta (2004), produced by Gagik Harutyunyan.
